reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart law dynamic, correctly note activated users are roughly flat, and converge on the same core fixes: pay on activation not signup, split the reward, segment referred vs organic, add fraud gates, and measure cost per activated user. Answer B adds a genuine extra diagnostic step - checking whether organic activation also dropped, which would point to a different root cause entirely. Answer A lacks this alternative-hypothesis check. Answer A's extra fraud-gate specifics are elaboration on a point B already covers. This makes B marginally stronger.
